commit.message:
Fix orchestrator: always run host discovery on shell access, including DCs

The orchestrator previously routed DCs exclusively to ad-discovery,
skipping host-level enumeration entirely. This missed additional NICs
(critical for pivoting), Hyper-V infrastructure, scheduled tasks with
stored creds, and local privilege escalation vectors.

Now: host discovery (windows-discovery/linux-discovery) runs on ALL
hosts when shell access is gained. On DCs, ad-discovery runs additionally
after host discovery completes. Sequential, not either/or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Support multi-chain transaction submission (#5)

## Motivation

We are running multiple instances of our consensus contract on different
chains and we want to relay transactions to all of these instances.

## Summary

Extend safenet-proxy to submit transaction attestations to multiple
consensus chains simultaneously from a single worker instance, rather
than being limited to a single configured chain.

## Key Changes

- **Configuration schema**: Replaced single-chain variables with
multi-chain equivalents:
  - `CHAIN_ID` → `CHAIN_IDS` (comma-separated list)
  - `RPC_URL` → `RPC_URLS` (JSON mapping of chain ID to RPC URL)
- `CONSENSUS_ADDRESS` → `CONSENSUS_ADDRESSES` (JSON mapping of chain ID
to contract address)
- Added cross-field validation to ensure all configured chains have
corresponding RPC URLs and consensus addresses

- **Queue consumer refactoring**: 
- Extracted per-chain submission logic into new `processChainMessages()`
function
- Parse all queue messages upfront, then submit each transaction to all
configured chains in parallel using `Promise.allSettled()`
- Each chain maintains its own nonce sequence within the batch
(baseNonce + messageIndex)
- Per-chain failures are logged independently without blocking other
chains
- All messages are acknowledged after attempting submission to all
chains (no-retry policy preserved)

- **Configuration files**:
- Updated `wrangler.jsonc` with new multi-chain variable format and
documentation
- Updated `src/index.ts` Bindings interface to reflect new environment
variable names
  - Updated `src/proposals/handler.ts` to use new variable names

- **Documentation**: Added `features/multi-network-transactions.md` with
implementation details, configuration examples, failure handling
semantics, and risk analysis

## Implementation Details

- Batch-level optimizations are preserved: EIP-1559 fees and account
nonce are fetched once per chain per batch
- Cross-chain submissions are parallelized, so latency scales with the
slowest chain rather than the sum
- The existing `SafeTransaction` struct already contains the originating
chain ID, so each consensus chain knows which network the transaction
came from
- Configuration validation happens at startup via Zod schema, catching
missing chain entries before any submissions occur

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Mwi33PR8Uq21wBSuY9eiNi

fix: robust large file handling in FFmpeg runners

- Cap stderr buffer at 3000 chars to prevent OOM on long jobs
  (FFmpeg prints per-frame progress — 100GB video = tens of MB of logs)

  (prevents killing jobs when Nginx proxy timeout fires mid-processing)
- Add X-Accel-Buffering: no header — tells Nginx not to buffer response,
  stream compressed video directly to client instead
- Add Content-Length header on all responses for proper download progress
- Log file sizes in MB for easier monitoring

commit.message:
Commit generated Prisma client; remove from .gitignore

Turbopack (Next.js 16) respects .gitignore and refuses to resolve
modules from gitignored paths — even if they exist on disk after
prisma generate runs. Committing src/generated/prisma/ ensures
Turbopack can always resolve the import.

Generated files are deterministic from the schema so committing
them is safe. prisma generate still runs during build to keep
them fresh after schema changes.

(pre-restore-TIMESTAMP.sql.gz). If safety backup fails, restore is
aborted — current data is never lost. If restore fails after drop,
the safety backup file is reported for manual recovery.

* feat: background pulse completion and tilt button pulse/toggle support

Pulse/toggle send methods now return immediately after the ON edge,
deferring the sleep+turn_off to a background task. This fixes calibration
overhead measuring as zero and improves position tracking accuracy.

Also adds _send_tilt_open/close/stop overrides to PulseModeCover and
ToggleModeCover so tilt buttons use the same pulse/toggle pattern as
travel buttons. Fixes ToggleModeCover.async_stop_cover missing the

* fix: set _last_command in _async_handle_command for toggle mode calibration

_async_handle_command dispatches to _send_open/_send_close but never set
_last_command. The higher-level methods (async_open/close_cover) set it,
but calibration calls _async_handle_command directly. Toggle mode's
_send_stop needs _last_command to know which button to re-pulse, so it
silently skipped every stop during calibration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: merge device_type/input_mode into control_mode and require stop switch for pulse mode

Replace the two-field device_type + input_mode system with a single
control_mode field (wrapped/switch/pulse/toggle). No migration needed
as this code hasn't been released yet.

Pulse mode now requires a stop switch entity — the entity shows as
unavailable in HA and the calibration tab is disabled until configured.
Tilt stop switch is also required for pulse mode with dual_motor tilt.

Tilt entity visibility now follows control mode:
- wrapped: no tilt entity pickers
- switch/toggle: tilt open + close
- pulse: tilt open + close + stop (required)

cv.positive_float behavior and prevents division-by-zero in the
position calculator and calibration logic.

commit.message:
fix: resolve user DB ID from backend to fix interview scheduling

AuthContext was storing the Cognito UUID as user.id, but multiple
components (InterviewScheduler, InterviewFeedbackForm, JobPostingForm,
etc.) expect a numeric database ID. Number(cognitoUUID) produces NaN,
causing "Unable to identify current user" errors.

After Cognito authentication, now calls /api/auth/me to fetch the
numeric DB user ID (provisioned by CognitoUserProvisioningFilter),
falling back to the Cognito UUID if the backend is unreachable.
